symbols.name: M
symbols.def: Number of clusters in the finite experiment.

symbols.name: m
symbols.def: Common number of units in each cluster.

symbols.name: N
symbols.def: Population size, N = M m.

symbols.name: C_1, ..., C_M
symbols.def: Partition of the finite population into M clusters of size m.

symbols.name: pbar
symbols.def: Target mean treatment saturation across clusters.

symbols.name: u
symbols.def: Generic cluster-level treatment saturation.

symbols.name: d
symbols.def: Centered saturation coordinate d = u - pbar.

symbols.name: delta_x
symbols.def: Dirac probability law at x.

symbols.name: nu
symbols.def: Continuous-relaxation law for first-stage cluster saturations.

symbols.name: pi
symbols.def: Finite design vector of realized cluster saturations.

symbols.name: hat_tau_DM
symbols.def: Difference-in-means estimator generated by the two-stage randomized saturation design.

symbols.name: tau
symbols.def: Average direct-effect contrast under the homogeneous linear-in-share working model at the target policy saturation pbar.

symbols.name: V_0
symbols.def: Constant term in the specialized Cai-style design-variance expansion.

symbols.name: V_1
symbols.def: Coefficient on the centered second moment m_2(nu).

symbols.name: V_3
symbols.def: Coefficient on the centered third moment m_3(nu).

symbols.name: V_4
symbols.def: Coefficient on the centered fourth-moment correction m_4(nu) - m_2(nu)^2.

symbols.name: s
symbols.def: Candidate centered second moment s = m_2(nu).

symbols.name: a, b, c
symbols.def: Dual/KKT multipliers for the profiled bounded-support moment program.

symbols.name: C_rd(nu)
symbols.def: Lipschitz constant controlling the change in V after perturbing the support masses by O(M^(-1)) and support points by O(m^(-1)).

symbols.name: Y_{ji}(z,u)
symbols.def: Potential outcome for unit i in cluster j under own treatment z and cluster saturation u.

symbols.name: T_pbar
symbols.def: Centering map T_pbar(u) = u - pbar.

symbols.name: D_pbar
symbols.def: Centered support domain D_pbar = [-pbar, 1 - pbar].

symbols.name: N(pbar)
symbols.def: Class of admissible saturation laws with mean pbar.

symbols.name: N_s(pbar,s)
symbols.def: Slice of admissible laws with centered second moment equal to s.

symbols.name: m_r(nu)
symbols.def: Centered r-th saturation moment of nu for r in {2,3,4}.

symbols.name: V(nu)
symbols.def: Quartic design-variance functional induced by the specialized homogeneous linear-in-share working model.

symbols.name: F(s)
symbols.def: Profiled inner value obtained by minimizing the quartic integrand over N_s(pbar,s).

symbols.name: Q_{a,b,c}(d)
symbols.def: Quartic dual residual polynomial indexed by the multiplier tuple (a,b,c).

symbols.name: G_{a,c}(s)
symbols.def: Outer quadratic certificate function G_{a,c}(s) = V_0 + a + c s - V_4 s^2 indexed by (a,c).

symbols.name: Pi_{M,m}(pbar)
symbols.def: Class of exact finite saturation vectors on the 1/m grid with mean pbar.

symbols.name: L_M(pi)
symbols.def: Empirical saturation law induced by the finite vector pi.

symbols.name: pi_rd
symbols.def: Finite saturation vector produced by the grid-split rounding construction.

symbols.name: nu_w
symbols.def: Witness law nu_w = (2/3) delta_0 + (1/3) delta_1 used in the skewed strict-improvement example.

assumptions.condition: The finite population is partitioned into M clusters C_1, ..., C_M of common size m.
Potential outcomes for units in cluster C_j depend only on assignments within C_j and not on assignments in C_l for l != j.
Within each cluster, the working-model contribution of a realized assignment to the variance of hat_tau_DM depends on the assignment only through the cluster treatment share u.

novel.justification: This is realistic for two-stage saturation studies in which peer exposure is deliberately coarsened to saturation shares and cluster composition is approximately homogeneous, such as village or classroom interventions designed around anonymous within-cluster mixing.

assumptions.condition: The specialized within-cluster working model is homogeneous and linear in the centered share d = u - pbar, so the Cai-style design variance of hat_tau_DM is determined by finitely many centered share moments and coefficients V_0, V_1, V_3, V_4 that do not vary across clusters.

novel.justification: This is satisfied in isolated partial-interference experiments whose design stage is calibrated to a homogeneous linear-in-share approximation, the exact specialization already suggested by Cai, Pouget-Abadie, and Airoldi as the tractable regime where saturation-moment tradeoffs become analytically interpretable.

assumptions.condition: The finite-population sequence used to define the randomized-saturation continuous-relaxation criterion is in the Cai-Pouget-Abadie-Airoldi asymptotic variance-expansion domain: M -> infinity, m -> infinity, the linear-interference potential-outcome parameters are uniformly bounded, and the regularity hypotheses of Cai, Pouget-Abadie, and Airoldi (2022, Theorem 9) hold in the homogeneous/block-fixed isolated-cluster specialization.

novel.justification: standard: Cai, Pouget-Abadie, and Airoldi (2022), Theorem 9 and Corollary 12, randomized-saturation linear-interference variance expansion

definitions.name: Admissible saturation laws
definitions.construction: For pbar in [0,1], N(pbar) = {nu in P([0,1]) : integral_[0,1] u d nu(u) = pbar}.

definitions.name: Centered saturation moments
definitions.construction: For r in {2,3,4}, m_r(nu) = integral_[0,1] (u - pbar)^r d nu(u).

definitions.name: Profiled inner value
definitions.construction: F(s) = inf_{nu in N_s(pbar,s)} integral_[0,1] [V_1 (u - pbar)^2 + V_3 (u - pbar)^3 + V_4 (u - pbar)^4] d nu(u).

definitions.name: Outer quadratic certificate
definitions.construction: For the necessary-and-sufficient global certificate, the outer certificate is the profiled value H(s) = V_0 + F(s) - V_4 s^2. The affine expression G^{lin}_{a,c}(s)=V_0+a+c s-V_4s^2 is only a sufficient lower-certificate obtained from one slice dual line; it is not necessary for global optimality.

definitions.name: Implementable saturation vectors
definitions.construction: For pbar in [0,1], Pi_{M,m}(pbar) = {pi in {0, 1/m, 2/m, ..., 1}^M : M^(-1) sum_{j=1}^M pi_j = pbar}.

definitions.name: Empirical law of a finite design
Grid-split rounding handle
definitions.construction: Given a K-atomic law nu = sum_{ell=1}^K w_ell delta_{u_ell}, first round the masses w_ell to a vector of cluster counts summing to M, then replace each support point u_ell by one or two adjacent points in {0,1/m,...,1} with mixing weights chosen to preserve the contribution of that atom to the mean constraint up to the final balancing adjustment that enforces M^(-1) sum_j pi_j = pbar.

definitions.name: Support-pattern contact system
definitions.construction: Let Theta={theta=(pbar,V_1,V_3,V_4):0<=pbar<=1}. For k in {1,2,3}, define E_k(theta) as the set of tuples (w_1,...,w_k,d_1,...,d_k) satisfying w_i>=0, sum_i w_i=1, -pbar<=d_1<=...<=d_k<=1-pbar, and sum_i w_i d_i=0. Define the branch objective R_k^0(theta,w,d)=V_1 sum_i w_i d_i^2+V_3 sum_i w_i d_i^3+V_4{sum_i w_i d_i^4-(sum_i w_i d_i^2)^2}; equivalently one may add the common constant V_0, which does not affect argmin comparisons. A tuple (theta,w,d,k) is globally active when (w,d) in E_k(theta) and, for every l in {1,2,3} and every (w',d') in E_l(theta), R_k^0(theta,w,d)<=R_l^0(theta,w',d'). Apply Tarski-Seidenberg quantifier elimination, followed by cylindrical algebraic decomposition with Thom encodings, to the finite family of active-branch formulas. This yields a finite semialgebraic partition Theta=union_r C_r and, on each cell C_r, a selected support size k_r and Thom-encoded algebraic branch functions w_{ri}(theta),d_{ri}(theta). The branch optimizer map is B(theta)=sum_{i=1}^{k_r} w_{ri}(theta) delta_{pbar+d_{ri}(theta)} for theta in C_r, with zero-weight atoms removed and duplicate support points merged.

statements.statement: Under ass:equal-cluster-size, ass:isolated-partial-interference, ass:anonymous-share-specialization, ass:homogeneous-linear-share-working-model, and ass:cai-leading-expansion-domain, for every nu in N(pbar), V(nu) is the standard finite-population design-variance criterion for first-stage saturation optimization: along finite randomized-saturation sequences whose saturation empirical law converges to nu, the o(N^{-1})-free leading term in Cai, Pouget-Abadie, and Airoldi's randomized-saturation variance expansion equals V(nu), with the V_2 coefficient equal to zero in the isolated homogeneous specialization. Thus the design-variance optimization target in this regime is exactly the quartic moment functional V; the separate non-leading finite-population variance is outside this criterion-level statement.
statements.justification: The proposal inherits the standard Cai-Pouget-Abadie-Airoldi leading design-variance object and identifies its homogeneous isolated-share specialization as the quartic moment functional V.
statements.gap: CaiPougetAbadieAiroldi2022 derives a broader linear-interference variance expression and studies optimization in structured design families; the exact quartic bounded-support objective is not isolated as a standalone theorem object for this homogeneous-within-cluster specialization.
statements.consumer: This lemma turns standard leading design-variance optimization into a fully explicit bounded-support moment optimization problem.

statements.statement: For nu_star in N(pbar), let s_star = m_2(nu_star), and write H(s)=V_0+F(s)-V_4 s^2 for the profiled outer value from def:outer-certificate. Then nu_star belongs to argmin_{nu in N(pbar)} V(nu) if and only if there exist scalars a_star, b_star, c_star such that Q_{a_star,b_star,c_star}(d) >= 0 for all d in D_pbar, Q_{a_star,b_star,c_star}(d) = 0 for every d in T_pbar(supp(nu_star)), and H(s) >= V(nu_star) for all s in [0, pbar(1-pbar)] with equality at s = s_star.
statements.justification: The kernel must be a true global certificate for the nonconvex moment program, not a local first-order condition that drops the outer m_2^2 term.
statements.gap: CaiPougetAbadieAiroldi2022 optimizes within structured design families, while the archived proposal failed because its KKT condition ignored the nonlinear -V_4 m_2(nu)^2 residual; no published saturation-design paper gives a necessary-and-sufficient certificate over all bounded-support laws with fixed mean.
statements.consumer: A referee or practitioner can verify optimality of any candidate saturation law by checking one quartic residual on the interval and one scalar outer inequality.

statements.statement: For the quartic saturation objective V over the admissible law class N(pbar), there exists nu_star in argmin_{nu in N(pbar)} V(nu) such that card(supp(nu_star)) <= 3.
statements.justification: A finite-atomic optimizer reduction is what converts the infinite-dimensional law problem into a tractable exact-design theorem.
statements.gap: Winkler1988 and Pinelis2012 provide extreme-point geometry for linear moment slices, but the published saturation-design literature does not combine that slice-wise result with the profiled nonlinear outer problem to deduce support-at-most-three for the actual quartic objective.
statements.consumer: This reduction feeds both the constructive skewed optimizer corollary and the finite-M implementation theorem.

statements.statement: For all real V_1, V_3, V_4 and every 0 < pbar < 1, delta_pbar belongs to argmin_{nu in N(pbar)} V(nu) if and only if min_{d in D_pbar} (V_1 + V_3 d + V_4 d^2) >= 0.
statements.justification: The main corollary should say exactly when the stratified benchmark survives interference in the only branch where the symmetric candidate is algebraically defensible.
statements.gap: CaiPougetAbadieAiroldi2022 shows endpoint optimality can fail under interference but does not derive this global iff threshold, and the archived versions overclaimed threshold statements outside the V_1 > 0, V_4 > 0 branch.
statements.consumer: A designer who wants to keep within-cluster stratification gets a closed inequality deciding whether that benchmark is globally safe.

statements.statement: At pbar = 1/3, V_1 = 1, V_3 = -10, and V_4 = 1, the law nu_w = (2/3) delta_0 + (1/3) delta_1 satisfies m_2(nu_w) = 2/9, m_3(nu_w) = 2/27, m_4(nu_w) - m_2(nu_w)^2 = 2/81, and V(nu_w) - V(delta_(1/3)) = -40/81 < 0.
statements.justification: A concrete strict-improvement witness prevents the positive-penalty threshold from reading like a purely symbolic branch condition.
statements.gap: The existing saturation-design papers do not display a finite feasible skewed law that beats the stratified benchmark by an explicit algebraic amount in this specialized quartic objective.
statements.consumer: The witness gives an immediately deployable counterexample showing why symmetric or endpoint heuristics can fail sharply.

statements.statement: Let nu = sum_{ell=1}^K w_ell delta_{u_ell} be in N(pbar). If M w_ell is an integer for every ell and m u_ell is an integer for every ell, then there exists pi in Pi_{M,m}(pbar) such that L_M(pi) = nu. In particular, these divisibility conditions already imply N pbar in Z. For the Bernoulli vertex (1 - pbar) delta_0 + pbar delta_1, exact attainability reduces to M pbar in Z. For the within-cluster stratified law delta_pbar, exact attainability reduces to m pbar in Z.
statements.justification: A continuous optimizer is only useful for experiment design if the law can be translated into an exact finite two-stage randomization when denominators line up.
statements.gap: CaiPougetAbadieAiroldi2022 passes to asymptotics and BasseFeller2016 studies finite two-stage analysis, but neither paper states the exact denominator-compatible realization theorem with separate cluster-mass and within-cluster-grid clauses.
statements.consumer: This theorem tells an experimentalist when the continuous optimum can be run exactly rather than merely approximated.

statements.statement: For every nu_star in N(pbar) with card(supp(nu_star)) <= 3 and N pbar in Z, the grid-split rounding handle produces pi_rd in Pi_{M,m}(pbar) such that V(L_M(pi_rd)) <= V(nu_star) + C_rd(nu_star) (M^(-1) + m^(-1)).
statements.justification: The finite-design theorem should not stop at exact compatibility because most empirical budgets land off the exact denominator grid.
statements.gap: The randomized-saturation literature discusses finite designs and large-cluster approximations, but it does not provide an optimizer-preserving O(M^(-1)+m^(-1)) loss bound for rounding a continuous quartic optimum.
statements.consumer: This gives a rate-certified recipe for implementing near-optimal skewed saturation laws in real experiments with finite M and m.

statements.statement: If V_3 = 0 and V_4 = 0, then V(nu) = V_0 + V_1 m_2(nu) for every nu in N(pbar). Hence V_1 > 0 implies delta_pbar minimizes V over nu in N(pbar), V_1 < 0 implies (1 - pbar) delta_0 + pbar delta_1 minimizes V over nu in N(pbar), and V_1 = 0 makes every nu in N(pbar) tie in V, hence all extremal-m_2 laws tie accordingly. The exact finite realization of the Bernoulli vertex uses M pbar in Z, whereas the exact finite realization of delta_pbar uses m pbar in Z.
statements.justification: The new interference theorem should collapse to the known SUTVA endpoint rule with the correct finite compatibility clauses.
statements.gap: CaiPougetAbadieAiroldi2022 proves the SUTVA endpoint comparison as a standalone result, but the present contribution is the noncircular reduction from the same quartic certificate together with the corrected integrality split.
statements.consumer: This sanity reduction is the benchmark a referee will use to verify that the interference theorem reproduces the established no-interference corner.

statements.statement: Certified delivered content plus the remaining atlas-compression question. Over Theta = {(pbar, V_1, V_3, V_4) : 0 <= pbar <= 1}, thm:constructive-optimal-design-algorithm gives a terminating exact algebraic procedure that outputs a globally optimal saturation law and certifies it; lem:explicit-profile-value gives a closed-form profiled H(s); and prop:explicit-two-point-cell gives the complete closed-form optimizer over the singleton/two-point class, with the exact algebraic comparison used by the constructive algorithm to decide global activation. The remaining open question is to simplify the full constructive/CAD output into a human-readable symbolic region-by-region atlas with compact interpretable inequalities and closed-form formulas for every three-point cell.
statements.justification: The honest residual is the symbolic simplification of the atlas, not existence, computability, or construction of an optimal design.
statements.gap: No published paper maps the full coefficient space of the quartic saturation objective into compact human-readable three-point optimizer regions; the delivered exact algorithm already computes and certifies the optimizer.
statements.consumer: A full symbolic atlas would be a lookup table, while the current theorem already gives an exact constructive optimizer.

statements.statement: Under ass:equal-cluster-size, ass:isolated-partial-interference, ass:anonymous-share-specialization, ass:homogeneous-linear-share-working-model, and ass:cai-leading-expansion-domain, by lem:variance-moment-reduction the standard leading design-variance-optimal saturation law over N(pbar) is exactly the V-minimizer. Hence leading design-variance optimality is characterized by thm:global-certificate. If 0 < pbar < 1, then the stratified design delta_pbar is leading-design-variance-optimal if and only if min_{d in D_pbar} (V_1 + V_3 d + V_4 d^2) >= 0 by prop:positive-penalty-threshold; if pbar in {0,1}, then N(pbar) = {delta_pbar}, so delta_pbar is automatically leading-design-variance-optimal. The SUTVA degeneration is given by prop:sutva-corner. The exact non-leading finite-population variance remains a separate scope item.
statements.justification: This corollary is the single design-level payoff that applies the modeling bridge to the pure moment-program theorems.
statements.gap: The structural gap is not a missing optimization theorem, but the need to keep modeling assumptions attached only to the criterion bridge and its design consequence.
statements.consumer: A design reader gets the leading-criterion optimizer rule without importing modeling assumptions into the abstract V-optimality statements.

statements.statement: For 0 < pbar < 1, write p=pbar, q=1-p, C=pq, and A=q-p. For s in [0,C], let I_2(s)=[s/p-p, q-s/q] and I_3(s)=[-s/q, s/p]. Define Phi_2(s,t)=V_1 s+V_4 s^2+s(V_3 t+V_4 t^2) and Phi_3(s,r)=V_1 s+V_3(A s+(s-C)r)+V_4((1-3C)s+(s-C)(r^2+A r)). Then the fixed-second-moment profile satisfies F(0)=0 and, for all s in [0,C], F(s)=min{min_{t in I_2(s)} Phi_2(s,t), min_{r in I_3(s)} Phi_3(s,r)}; consequently H(s)=V_0+F(s)-V_4s^2. The first minimum is attained by the two-point centered law whose support points are the roots of x^2-tx-s=0. The second is attained by the endpoint-interior law on {-p,r,q} with weights (s+rq)/(p+r), (C-s)/((p+r)(q-r)), and (s-pr)/(q-r), with the endpoint limiting interpretation. Since the two displayed minimizations are quadratic over closed intervals, F and H are explicit piecewise-algebraic functions of (s,pbar,V_1,V_3,V_4).
statements.justification: The profiled certificate becomes a computable closed formula once the fixed-second-moment slice is reduced to the two exposed moment-boundary families.
statements.gap: The prior version left F(s) implicit; moment-set references give support bounds, but not this saturation-specific profile formula.
statements.consumer: This removes the hidden outer-profile dependency from thm:global-certificate and makes H(s) directly evaluable.

statements.statement: There is a complete, exact, terminating constructive optimizer for the quartic saturation program. The input is theta=(pbar,V_1,V_3,V_4) with 0<=pbar<=1, and the output is a law nu_alg in N(pbar) minimizing V. If pbar is 0 or 1, output delta_pbar; this boundary case is certified directly because N(pbar) is then the singleton {delta_pbar}. If 0<pbar<1, write p=pbar, q=1-p, C=pq, and A=q-p, then enumerate the finite support/contact subsystems: S1, the singleton centered support {0}; S2, the two-point centered supports {-b,a}, equivalently the endpoint cubic branches g_L(t)=p(p+t)(V_1+V_3t+V_4t^2) on [-p,A] and g_R(t)=q(q-t)(V_1+V_3t+V_4t^2) on [A,q] together with their endpoint and stationary equations; and S3, the three-point endpoint-interior centered supports {-p,r,q}, equivalently the KKT systems for the explicit profile branch Phi_3 over 0<=s<=C and -s/q<=r<=s/p, including every active boundary subset. Solve these finitely many polynomial systems by exact real-algebraic elimination, cylindrical algebraic decomposition, Thom encodings, and root isolation; discard infeasible roots; construct the associated laws and exact algebraic V-values; and choose a minimum. The chosen law is certified by exact candidate comparison and, on interior contact patterns, by constructing the quartic residual Q, checking Q>=0 on D_pbar by Sturm/root isolation, and checking the outer inequality H(s)>=V(nu_alg) on [0,C] using the explicit closed-form H(s)=V_0+F(s)-V_4s^2. Exact algebraic CAD/Thom computation is the constructive method; the separate atlas question is only whether this exact output can be compressed into compact human-readable regions.
statements.justification: A theorem about optimal saturation design should output an actual optimal law; the exact algebraic solver is the constructive optimizer and certificate.
statements.gap: The existing branch-map node supplied CAD/Thom existence-level selection but not an explicit terminating construction that a designer can run from the four primitives.
statements.consumer: Given (pbar,V_1,V_3,V_4), this theorem terminates, returns a globally optimal saturation law, and certifies it.

statements.statement: The exact optimizer over the singleton/two-point saturation class is closed form. Let p=pbar, q=1-p, A=q-p, and q_theta(t)=V_1+V_3t+V_4t^2. The value above V_0 is the minimum of 0, the left endpoint cubic branch g_L(t)=p(p+t)q_theta(t) on t in [-p,A], and the right endpoint cubic branch g_R(t)=q(q-t)q_theta(t) on t in [A,q]. The complete candidate set is t=-p,A,q together with the roots in [-p,A] of V_1+pV_3+2(V_3+pV_4)t+3V_4t^2=0 and the roots in [A,q] of -V_1+qV_3+2(qV_4-V_3)t-3V_4t^2=0. Choosing the candidate with smallest value gives the two-point-class optimum: value 0 gives delta_pbar; a left-branch minimizer gives centered support {-p,p+t} with weights (p+t)/(2p+t) at -p and p/(2p+t) at p+t; and a right-branch minimizer gives centered support {t-q,q} with weights q/(2q-t) at t-q and (q-t)/(2q-t) at q. The two-versus-three-point comparison is the exact algebraic comparison performed in thm:constructive-optimal-design-algorithm and recorded in oeq:full-branch-optimizer-map; it is not a missing piece of this complete two-point result.
statements.justification: The practically relevant skewed design should have an inspectable formula rather than only a black-box algebraic description.
statements.gap: Prior randomized-saturation design results optimize over restricted menus but do not give this endpoint-skewed two-atom KKT solution inside the unrestricted law program.
statements.consumer: A practitioner can compute the exact singleton/two-point optimum by checking two endpoint cubic branches and at most two quadratic stationary equations, then use the constructive algorithm for the separate global comparison.

target_estimand: tau = M^(-1) sum_{j=1}^M m^(-1) sum_{i in C_j} [Y_{ji}(1,pbar) - Y_{ji}(0,pbar)]

sampling_model.design: Finite-population two-stage randomized saturation design under isolated partial interference; the first-stage design law nu chooses cluster saturations and the second stage randomizes binary treatment within clusters at the realized saturation.
sampling_model.units: N = M m fixed units partitioned into M equal-size clusters C_1, ..., C_M.
sampling_model.treatment: Binary treatment assigned within cluster j according to its realized saturation u or finite-grid counterpart pi_j.
sampling_model.overlap: Design positivity is generated by the support of nu or pi over admissible saturation levels with mean pbar.
sampling_model.nuisance_estimation: None. The proposal is design-based on a fixed finite population with a specialized homogeneous linear-in-share working variance expansion.

bibliography.citation: Hudgens, Michael G., and Elizabeth Halloran (2008). "Toward Causal Inference with Interference." Journal of the American Statistical Association 103(482): 832-842.
Basse, Guillaume, and Avi Feller (2016). "Analyzing Two-Stage Experiments in the Presence of Interference." arXiv:1608.06805.
Baird, Sarah, J. Aislinn Bohren, Craig McIntosh, and Berk Ozler (2017). "Optimal Design of Experiments in the Presence of Interference." Working paper.
Cai, Chencheng, Jean Pouget-Abadie, and Edoardo M. Airoldi (2022). "Optimizing Randomized and Deterministic Saturation Designs under Interference." arXiv:2203.09682.
Offer-Westort, Molly, and Drew Dimmery (2021). "Experimentation for Homogeneous Policy Change." arXiv:2101.12318.
Pinelis, Iosif (2012). "On the Extreme Points of Moments Sets." arXiv:1204.0249.
Winkler, Gerhard (1988). "Extreme Points of Moment Sets." Mathematics of Operations Research 13(4): 581-587.
Lu, Sizhu, Lei Shi, and Peng Ding (2026). "Estimating Within-Cluster and Between-Cluster Spillover Effects in Randomized Saturation Designs." arXiv:2603.19573.

comparator_promise_table.comparator_claim: Derives closed-form bias and variance under a linear interference model, optimizes randomized designs analytically within a symmetric treatment-proportion family, and proposes deterministic saturation designs under model specification.
Maps randomized saturation designs into regression and power calculations over finite saturation supports and studies associated design tradeoffs.
Develops design-based analysis for finite two-stage experiments with interference and practical implementation complications.
Shows that interior treatment-correlation designs can dominate one-stage endpoint designs for homogeneous-policy effects under partial interference.
Shows that extreme points of generalized moment sets are finitely atomic with support size bounded by the number of moment restrictions.
Develops extreme-point geometry for moment problems that reduces linear optimization over moment sets to finitely atomic laws.
Extends randomized saturation inference to within-cluster and between-cluster spillovers under an exposure-mapping framework.

tldr: The proposal studies standard leading first-stage design in two-stage randomized-saturation experiments by separating the Cai-Pouget-Abadie-Airoldi design-variance criterion from the abstract moment program. Under the equal-cluster homogeneous isolated-share specialization, lem:variance-moment-reduction identifies the o(N^-1)-free leading design-variance criterion with the quartic moment functional V(nu). The delivered optimization content is a global dual/KKT certificate for V, a closed-form threshold for the stratified law delta_pbar, a complete closed-form optimizer over the singleton/two-point class, a support-at-most-three optimizer reduction, finite-M attainability with O(M^(-1)+m^(-1)) rounding implementation, and a terminating exact algebraic optimizer/certificate procedure. The remaining directions are the compact human-readable symbolic atlas and exact non-leading finite-population variance, recorded as scope rather than defects.

project_justification.gap: Randomized-saturation papers derive leading variance expressions and optimize inside finite or structured design families, but they do not bridge the Cai continuous-relaxation leading criterion to a full bounded-support quartic law program and then certify its global optimizer.
project_justification.niche: The live niche is the exact design geometry of the isolated homogeneous within-cluster leading criterion: a profiled nonconvex moment problem sitting strictly between generic moment-set theory and applied randomized-saturation heuristics.
project_justification.fill: The project provides the bridge from the standard leading design-variance criterion to V, proves the global dual/KKT certificate, gives the closed-form threshold for the stratified delta_pbar design, proves the complete singleton/two-point optimizer formula, proves support-at-most-three, supplies finite-M attainability and O(M^(-1)+m^(-1)) rounding, and gives a terminating exact algebraic optimizer/certificate procedure.

related_work: Hudgens and Halloran (2008) introduced the two-stage partial-interference design framework and the causal contrasts that make randomized saturation meaningful. Baird, Bohren, McIntosh, and Ozler (2017) place randomized saturation in a regression and power-calculation framework and derive design tradeoffs over finite saturation menus, but they do not solve the full bounded-support law problem. Basse and Feller (2016) study finite two-stage analysis with unequal cluster sizes, weighting, and variance estimation; the present proposal instead contributes an optimizer-preserving attainability and rounding theory. Cai, Pouget-Abadie, and Airoldi (2022) are the closest comparator: they derive closed-form bias and variance under a linear interference model, optimize randomized designs analytically within a symmetric treatment-proportion family, and propose deterministic saturation designs under correct model specification; the current angle makes the unrestricted bounded-support moment program induced by the homogeneous isolated-share leading criterion itself the theorem object. Offer-Westort and Dimmery (2021) show that interior treatment-correlation designs can beat endpoint rules for homogeneous-policy estimands, reinforcing that the endpoint benchmark is not structurally safe. Pinelis (2012) and Winkler (1988) supply the finite-atomic geometry for linear moment slices, but they do not handle the profiled outer nonlinearity created by -V_4 m_2(nu)^2. Lu, Shi, and Ding (2026) mark the current frontier in broader spillover settings and clarify that the present proposal is a deliberately specialized exact-design theorem under isolated within-cluster interference rather than a solution to general network experimentation.
honest_scope: The modeling assumptions enter through the standard leading design-variance bridge and the design-optimality corollary; the global certificate, stratified-threshold theorem, singleton/two-point optimizer, and SUTVA corner are pure statements about the quartic V program. The closed-form threshold is for the stratified law delta_pbar specifically, with the skewed witness showing why that branch can fail. The branch solver delivers a complete exact algebraic optimizer and certificate, and prop:explicit-two-point-cell fully solves the singleton/two-point class; the exhaustive human-readable region-by-region symbolic atlas and the exact non-leading finite-population variance are the residual directions.
